originalTitle: France returns sacred 'talking drum' looted during colonial rule to Ivory Coast
title: France returns sacred 'talking drum' looted during colonial rule to Ivory Coast
content: The move forms part of a broader process of returning cultural artefacts to African countries that started in 2017.‎

originalTitle: Cartoonist freed after 15 years in prison without charge in Eritrea
title: A Happy Day for Artist Biniam! He is Free!
content:
A Happy Day for Biniam!

Hello, super friends from Africa and all over! I have some truly wonderful news to share with you today. It's a story about a special artist from a country called Eritrea, right here in Africa. His name is Biniam Solomon, and he is finally free! For a very, very long time, Biniam was in prison. But now, he is out! This is such happy news for him, his family, and everyone who cares about artists and stories. Let's learn more about Biniam and why this is so special.

Who is Biniam Solomon?

Biniam Solomon is an amazing artist. He is a cartoonist! Do you know what a cartoonist does? A cartoonist draws pictures, often funny ones, that tell a story or share an idea. Imagine drawing a silly cat or a brave hero – that's what cartoonists do! Biniam was very, very good at drawing. His cartoons were not just funny; they were smart! They made people think about important things happening around them. People in Eritrea loved to see his drawings in the newspapers. His cartoons helped people understand things in a simple way. They could make you laugh, but also make you think, "Hmm, that's a good point!" *Did you know?* Cartoons are like magic drawings! They can talk to us without saying a single word, just by showing us pictures.

What Happened Long Ago?

Many years ago, in Biniam's home country of Eritrea, something sad happened. The places where people could write and share news and ideas, like newspapers, were closed down. This is sometimes called when the "free press" stopped. It meant that people could not easily share their thoughts or stories with everyone. The government didn't want some ideas to be shared. Because Biniam drew cartoons that made people think, he was taken to prison. This happened when he was a young man, full of bright ideas and funny drawings. It was a difficult time for many artists and writers in Eritrea.

15 Very Long Years

Can you imagine being away from your family, your friends, and your home for a long, long time? Biniam Solomon was in prison for fifteen whole years! That is an incredibly long time. It is longer than most of you have even been alive! For 15 years, he couldn't draw his amazing pictures. He missed birthdays, holidays, and all the everyday moments with his loved ones. It must have been very, very hard and lonely. And the trickiest part? He was in prison "without charge." This means no one ever told him what wrong thing he did! No one said, "Biniam, you broke this rule." He was just kept there, and that is not fair at all. *Did you know?* Fifteen years is like waiting for 15 Christmases or 15 Eid celebrations! That's a lot of missed fun and family time.

Where is Eritrea?

Eritrea is a beautiful country in Africa. It is in East Africa, right next to the big, blue Red Sea. It's a place with sunny skies, pretty mountains, and kind people. It has a rich history and culture, with delicious food and old stories. Children there play games and go to school, just like you do. It's a vibrant part of our amazing continent. Sometimes, even in lovely places, things can be tricky for a while. But people always hope for brighter days and more freedom for everyone in Eritrea. *Did you know?* Eritrea is home to the ancient port city of Massawa, which has beautiful old buildings and a fascinating history near the sea.

The Happy News! Biniam is Free!

Now, after all those years, the wonderful news has arrived: Biniam Solomon is free! He is no longer in prison. Can you hear the cheers and see the smiles? Imagine the joy of seeing your family after so many years! Big hugs, happy tears, and endless stories to share. He can finally breathe fresh, open air and feel the warm sunshine without walls around him. This is a moment of great happiness for Biniam, his family, and everyone who believes in fairness and freedom. He can finally go home and maybe even start drawing again!

Why are Cartoons So Important?

You might think cartoons are just for fun and giggles, and they often are! But they are also very, very important. Cartoons can help us learn new things in a fun way. They can make us laugh when we feel sad. More than that, cartoons can help us think about how we can make our world a better place. Biniam's cartoons were special because they showed big ideas in simple pictures. This is a powerful gift! When artists like Biniam share their ideas through drawings, it helps everyone understand things better. It helps us talk about important stuff, even if it's tricky. It brings people together. *Did you know?* Many superheroes you see in movies, like Spider-Man or Black Panther, started as drawings in comic books! They also tell stories and share important ideas.

What Happens Next for Biniam?

We don't know exactly what Biniam will do now that he is free. Maybe he will draw many, many new pictures! Perhaps he will spend lots of time with his family, catching up on everything he missed. We hope he finds lots of joy and peace after such a long and difficult time. We hope he can use his amazing talent to draw and share stories again, making people think and smile. His freedom reminds us that even when things are very hard and take a long time, good things can still happen. It gives us hope for other people who might be in similar situations.

Let's Be Kind and Share Our Stories!

This story of Biniam Solomon reminds us to be kind to everyone and to always believe in fairness. It also reminds us that sharing our ideas and listening to others is very, very important. Every voice and every picture matters. When we share stories, whether through words or drawings, we learn and grow together. When we draw pictures, we make the world more colorful and interesting. So, keep drawing, keep sharing your thoughts, and keep hoping for happy days for everyone in Africa and all over the world! Keep smiling and keep drawing, super artists! Your art can make a difference, just like Biniam's.
summary: Biniam Solomon, a kind artist from Eritrea who draws cartoons, is finally free after being in prison for 15 very long years without anyone saying what wrong thing he did.
whyMatters: This is important because it shows that even after a long, unfair time, good things can happen, and that artists like Biniam help us think and share ideas, which is important for everyone.
